videos.title: Units of Length | Centimetres, Metres, and Kilometres for Kids
videos.description: Learn about the three main units of length: centimetres (cm), metres (m), and kilometres (km). Discover when to use each unit with fun everyday examples!
learningObjectives: Know the three units of length: cm, m, km
Understand when to use each unit

videos.title: How to Use a Ruler and Measuring Tape | Measuring Length for Kids
videos.description: Learn how to measure objects using a ruler and a measuring tape. See how to read centimetres and metres on different measuring tools.
learningObjectives: Use a measuring tape for longer objects
Read measurements in cm and m

videos.title: What is Measurement? | Introduction to Measuring for Kids
videos.description: Learn what measurement means and why we measure things. Understand that measurement tells us how long, how heavy, or how much something is.
learningObjectives: Apply measurement concepts to real life
Choose the correct unit for different objects
keyConceptsCovered: Why hand spans are not reliable

videos.title: How to Read a Ruler | Using a Ruler for Beginners
videos.description: Learn how to hold a ruler correctly, start measuring from zero, and read the markings. Perfect for beginners who are just starting to measure!

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Quick introduction to units and measuring tools for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete coverage of units and measurement basics

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Start with measurement review and ruler basics, then units of length

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites
after_prerequisite_review.rationale: Essential units of length introduction only

questions.question: Which unit would you use to measure the length of your classroom?
questions.explanation: We use metres to measure things like rooms and buildings. Centimetres are for small things and kilometres are for very long distances.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Start by letting students explore different measuring tools. Have rulers and measuring tapes available. Ask them to guess which unit they would use for different objects before watching the video.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: Help your child measure things around the house with a ruler and a measuring tape. Talk about when we use cm, m, and km in daily life.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Measurement is everywhere! Look around you and think about which unit you would use to measure things like your pencil, your room, and the road to your school.
